channel.title: Comments for page "To The Sickest Bastard i Knew"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "To The Sickest Bastard i Knew"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a very big fan of your tales, DSC. This is no different and is likely among your best work.

Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
intentional. i'm very deliberate as a person for uppercase/lowercase i's, where I indicates a strong sense of self or an assertion of self, and i is a weak/nonexistent sense of self or a denial of self.

here, it represents the main character's complete abandonment of previous self in the pursuit of a new, but undiscovered, self

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question about the title, You start each word with an uppercase letter but "i" which, aside from the first word, should be capitalised. Is that intentional and if so why?

item.title: author toast
item.content:encoded:
god dude.

i've been wanting to write a tale based on tufto's interpretation of the scarlet king for a while now.

originally this was going to be twin brothers having taken different paths to deal with witnessing the death of their mother, one becoming a child of the scarlet king himself and the other an insurgency operative.

this fell apart when i realized it's very fucking hard to develop a story structure which isn't fuckshit long for a story of that nature. it just wasn't worth the cost imo.

aside from that:

Y. P. stands for Yelizaveta Pavlova, which i understand to mean "Small pledge to God".

originally "nobody" was crosslinked to the nobody hub, but honestly i don't much think its of that nature. this isn't an origin story of "nobody", because "nobody" is a form of identity on the wiki. this is meant to indicate the abandonment of self and humanity.

im gay
